intro: Tolpis azorica, also known as Azores Tolpis, is a small, succulent perennial plant native to the Azores islands, characterized by its silvery-gray leaves and small yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to aid in the treatment of various skin conditions, such as eczema and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Tolpis azorica include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tolpis azorica is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used to treat skin issues, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
